PH1.text: Here's what every business owner sending money to China needs to know about hidden bank fees.

When you wire money to your supplier in China, your bank can charge you in two ways at the same time. One shows up on the receipt. The other hides inside the exchange rate. Most business owners only ever see the first one. We'll walk you through both.

PG4.template: Here's what every (audience) needs to know about (topic), explained simply.
PG4.text: Here's what every business owner needs to know about how cross-border wire pricing actually works.

Cross-border wires to China are priced in two layers. The first layer is the wire fee, the visible number on top. The second layer is the markup baked into the exchange rate, which is rarely listed anywhere on the receipt. We'll walk through both so you know what you're actually paying.

PG5.text: Here's why "fee waived" on your wire receipt doesn't mean the wire was free.

Your wire receipt says fee waived. You feel good. But here's what business owners often miss. The exchange rate the bank used to convert your dollars can be a few percent off the real rate, and that gap is where the actual cost lives. On a 10,000 dollar wire, that gap is roughly 300 dollars.
